cluster.title: Biological Maintenance — Kefiw
cluster.subhead: Fuel floor, decay curves, sleep windows, red-flag routing.
cluster.intro: Four instruments for the baseline upkeep of a biological chassis: your resting energy cost, the pharmacokinetics of caffeine and alcohol, the 90-minute sleep cycle, and the red-flag router that decides ER vs urgent care vs home.
cluster.description: BMR/TDEE calculator, caffeine and alcohol half-life plot, 90-minute sleep-cycle timing, and a symptom-based triage router.

faq.q: How accurate is BMR vs resting metabolic rate?
faq.a: Mifflin-St Jeor is ±10% vs indirect calorimetry for a healthy adult. Lab RMR testing narrows the band if precision matters.
No — it is a red-flag heuristic. When unsure, call the nurse line or emergency services. False-negative cost is orders of magnitude higher than false-positive cost.

tools.title: Metabolic Floor — BMR / TDEE Calculator | Kefiw

current.description: Calculate your Basal Metabolic Rate and Total Daily Energy Expenditure using Mifflin-St Jeor. Power-consumption view with cut / maintain / bulk zones.
current.intro: The minimum kcal your chassis burns at standby (BMR), and the total expenditure (TDEE) once you layer activity. Mifflin-St Jeor is the current gold-standard regression for resting metabolism — more accurate than Harris-Benedict by ~5%.
current.outcomeLine: Your chassis idle draw (BMR) and full-load TDEE, plus cut / maintain / bulk zones.
howTo: Enter sex, age, weight, and height.
Pick an activity multiplier — sedentary is actually sedentary (desk + no formal exercise).
Use Cut / Maintain / Bulk zones for calorie targets against your goal.

examples.title: 35y male · 80 kg · 180 cm · moderate
examples.body: BMR ≈ 1800 kcal · TDEE ≈ 2790 kcal · Cut 2290 · Bulk 3090.

faq.q: Why Mifflin-St Jeor instead of Harris-Benedict?
faq.a: Mifflin-St Jeor (1990) fits modern populations better — Harris-Benedict (1919) systematically over-estimates BMR for sedentary people.
±10–15% vs doubly-labelled water. The activity multiplier is the biggest source of error — most people over-estimate their activity level.

tools.title: Substance Decay — Caffeine & Alcohol Half-Life | Kefiw

current.description: Plot the half-life decay of caffeine or alcohol. See when the intake drops below the jitter threshold and the sleep-interference threshold.
current.intro: Caffeine half-life ≈ 5 h, alcohol ≈ 1.5 h (elimination, not BAC decline). Plots the exponential decay curve so you can see when the intake crosses the jitter line and the sleep-interference line.
current.outcomeLine: Plot caffeine or alcohol half-life decay against jitter and sleep thresholds.
howTo: Enter intake mg and hours since consumption.
The decay curve shows remaining mass; dashed lines mark jitter + sleep thresholds.

examples.title: 200 mg coffee at 2pm
examples.body: ~100 mg remaining at 7pm · ~50 mg at midnight — still above sleep-interference threshold.

faq.q: Why does caffeine feel worn off before it decays?
faq.a: Subjective alertness tracks the first half-life; residual caffeine still binds adenosine receptors and disrupts REM sleep for 6+ hours.
Ethanol elimination is zero-order (constant rate) once enzymes are saturated — roughly 10 g/h. For BAC over time, use Impairment Coefficient.

tools.title: REM-Sync — Sleep Cycle Calculator | Kefiw

current.description: Find the optimal bedtime or wake time based on 90-minute sleep cycles. Wake at the end of a cycle, not the middle.
current.intro: A full sleep cycle runs ~90 minutes. Waking at the end of a cycle (light NREM) feels rested; waking mid-REM or deep NREM feels groggy. Five to six cycles is the performance target.
current.outcomeLine: Sleep or wake at the end of a 90-min cycle, not mid-REM.
howTo: Pick mode — wake at a target time, sleep at a target clock time, or sleep now.
The tool lists 3–7 cycle windows with recommended bedtimes or wake times.
Optimal windows (5 or 6 cycles) glow cyan.

examples.title: Wake at 6:30 AM
examples.body: Optimal sleep: 9:36 PM (6 cycles) or 11:06 PM (5 cycles). Add ~14 min sleep latency.

faq.a: Yes — 75 to 110 minutes, individual and age-dependent. 90 min is the population mean used by most sleep studies.

faq.q: What if I can't sleep 5+ cycles?
faq.a: End on a full cycle. Three complete cycles (4.5 h) feels better than four partial ones (5 h cut mid-REM).

tools.title: Medical Triage — ER vs Urgent Care vs Home | Kefiw

current.description: Three-tier triage routing for common symptoms. Flags red symptoms to ER, amber to urgent care, green to self-care. Hard-coded heuristic only — not a diagnosis.
keywords: should I go to the ER
current.intro: Picks between ER, urgent care, and self-care based on published red-flag lists. Checks universal killers first (chest pain + arm radiation, FAST stroke signs, unresponsive, cyanosis) then symptom-specific flags.
current.outcomeLine: Three-tier red-flag router: ER, urgent care, or self-care — no diagnosis.
howTo: Check any red-flag boxes that apply.
Verdict: Level 1 (ER now), Level 2 (urgent care today), or Level 3 (self-care acceptable).

examples.title: Chest pain + arm radiation
examples.body: Verdict: Level 1. Call emergency services. Do not drive yourself.

faq.a: No. It is a routing heuristic based on published red-flag lists. It can miss atypical presentations. If unsure, call the nurse line or emergency services.
False-negative cost (missed MI, stroke, sepsis) is orders of magnitude higher than false-positive cost (unnecessary ER trip).

enhancement_brief.goal: Lift each article from short-form summary to authority-grade long-form. Target 1,200+ words per article while keeping the structured fields crisp.
per_article_outputs: 1. Fill `enhanced` with any structured-field overrides (usually: tighter intro, expanded keyPoints, more examples, richer FAQ).
2. Fill `longform_markdown` with 800-1200 word prose body (markdown, H2 sections only — no H1).
3. Do not modify `current` — that is the baseline snapshot for diffing.

rules: Each question must read like a real search query — not marketing-speak.
First sentence of answer is a complete standalone 15-25 word definition/answer (Google quotes it directly).
Full answer 40-60 words. Tight. Front-load the answer.
Cover distinct intents: definition, comparison, edge-case, how-to, troubleshooting. Not 3 rewordings of the same angle.
Include one target keyword in at least half the questions.
Add a faq_intent tag per entry: "definition" | "comparison" | "edge-case" | "how-to" | "troubleshooting" | "cost" | "trust"
faq_rules.target_count: 5-7 entries per article, 4-6 per tool page

structure: ## H2 section 1 — deeper "what is it" / definition with context
## H2 section 2 — the mechanic / how it works (with one small example)
## H2 section 3 — when to use vs when to avoid
## H2 section 4 — worked example or walkthrough
## H2 section 6 — tool-specific tips (link out to the tool)
longform_rules.tone: Concrete, useful, first-person-plural light. No filler. No "in conclusion". Short paragraphs.
links: Every time you mention a sibling tool in this cluster, link it with the exact URL from this brief (tools[].url).
Every time you mention a sibling article, link it with the exact URL from this brief (articles[].url).
Do not invent URLs or link to external competitors.
forbidden: Medical/legal/financial advice language ("you should", "we recommend") — use neutral framing.
Generic SEO boilerplate ("in today's fast-paced world").

internal_linking.rule: Use only URLs that appear in this JSON. Prefer linking to sibling tools/articles in THIS cluster over outbound links.

quality_gates: No fabricated facts — if uncertain, phrase as "in practice" or remove.
Every H2 section earns its space — cut if it restates the intro.
Zero AI-tells: "it is important to note", "in conclusion", "let us delve", "furthermore".
